ogTitle: Bowen Mountain vs Bowling Alley Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bowen Mountain and Bowling Alley Point.
heading: Bowen Mountain vs Bowling Alley Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bowen Mountain, New South Wales and Bowling Alley Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bowling Alley Point has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Bowen Mountain (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bowen Mountain or Bowling Alley Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bowen Mountain has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Bowling Alley Point scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bowen Mountain scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Bowling Alley Point scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bowen Mountain or Bowling Alley Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bowling Alley Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
